title: Exit Tickets
tagline: Cite Evidence | Support Claims | Analyze Text
objectives:
• Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly
• Make inferences based on evidence and draw logical conclusions from text
directions: Answer the question below in complete sentences.

tickets.title: Finding Explicit Evidence
tickets.question: What is one piece of textual evidence that directly supports the author's main claim? Include the quote and page number.
tickets.sample_answer: Students should identify a specific quote from the text that clearly states or supports the main argument. They must include proper citation with page numbers or paragraph references.

tickets.title: Making Text Inferences
tickets.question: What can you infer about the author's purpose based on the evidence presented? Support your inference with specific details.
tickets.sample_answer: Students should make a logical inference about why the author wrote the text. They need to connect their inference to specific textual evidence or examples from the reading.

tickets.title: Analyzing Supporting Details
tickets.question: Which piece of evidence best supports the central idea? Explain why this evidence is most convincing.
tickets.sample_answer: Students should identify the strongest piece of supporting evidence and explain its effectiveness. They should demonstrate understanding of how evidence relates to the main idea.

tickets.title: Drawing Conclusions
tickets.question: Based on the evidence in the text, what conclusion can you draw about the topic? Cite at least one supporting detail.
tickets.sample_answer: Students should form a logical conclusion based on textual evidence. They must include at least one specific detail or quote that led them to this conclusion.

tickets.title: Evidence Evaluation
tickets.question: How does the author use evidence to convince readers? Give one example of effective evidence from the text.
tickets.sample_answer: Students should analyze how the author presents evidence persuasively. They need to provide a specific example and explain why it effectively supports the author's argument.
